message.content: continus following onboarding -- I've set up global git user and email.  you were working on agentrail complete in a new feature or fix branch I think

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: git status && git branch --show-current && git log --oneline -5

content.content:
On branch feature/claude-md-branching
Changes to be committed:
  (use "git restore --staged <file>..." to unstage)
	modified:   CLAUDE.md

feature/claude-md-branching
7b5a9a4 feat(ops): multi-axis take and drop on matrices (Step 081)
f4958af test: automated test runner with expected output comparison (Step 079)
5fb1b50 feat(eval): vector bracket indexing — V[indices] selects multiple elements
89a4728 fix(eval): bracket indexing respects quad-origin (Step 078)
515a585 fix(ops): dyadic cat now handles boxed arrays (Step 077)

Saga: cor24-apl-interpreter
Status: active
Current step: 81
Created: 2026-03-29T16:24:30

Steps:
  [x] 001-setup-repo-structure [production]: Create the project directory structure and build system. Create src/ for C so...
  [x] 002-uart-io-bootstrap [legacy]: Write uart_putchar, uart_getchar, uart_puts, uart_getline with backspace support. Test echo and startup banner. (Phase 0, Step 0.2)
  [x] 003-number-io [legacy]: Write print_int and parse_int for decimal I/O with underscore-negative. Test round-trip. (Phase 0, Step 0.3)
  [x] 004-tokenizer [legacy]: Implement lexer with token types NUM, IDENT, RESERVED, operators, ASSIGN, parens. Reserved word lookup. (Phase 1, Step 1.1)
  [x] 005-scalar-parser [legacy]: Right-to-left recursive descent parser for scalar expressions with +, -, *, parens, monadic negate. (Phase 1, Step 1.2)
  [x] 006-scalar-evaluator [legacy]: Tree-walking evaluator for scalar arithmetic including software divide. (Phase 1, Step 1.3)
  [x] 007-scalar-repl [legacy]: Wire up REPL loop: read line, tokenize, parse, eval, print. Error recovery. (Phase 1, Step 1.4)
  [x] 008-variables [legacy]: Symbol table (64 entries), assignment with <-, variable references in expressions. (Phase 1, Step 1.5)
  [x] 009-array-data-structure [legacy]: Heap bump allocator, Array header struct, new_array/get/set accessors. (Phase 2, Step 2.1)
  [x] 010-vector-literals [legacy]: Parser recognizes adjacent numbers as vectors. Create vector nodes in AST. (Phase 2, Step 2.2)
  [x] 011-element-wise-ops [legacy]: Extend evaluator for vector+vector, scalar extension, conformability checks. (Phase 2, Step 2.3)
  [x] 012-vector-output [legacy]: Print vectors space-separated with consistent width. (Phase 2, Step 2.4)
  [x] 013-iota [legacy]: Implement monadic iota N generating 0..N-1 vector. (Phase 3, Step 3.1)
  [x] 014-rho [legacy]: Implement monadic rho (shape-of) and dyadic rho (reshape with cyclic fill). (Phase 3, Step 3.2)
  [x] 015-reduce [legacy]: Implement +/ -/ */ reduce operators over vectors, right-to-left. (Phase 3, Step 3.3)
  [x] 016-take-drop [legacy]: Implement dyadic take and drop with negative-N support. (Phase 3, Step 3.4)
  [x] 017-rev-cat [legacy]: Implement monadic rev (reverse), monadic cat (ravel), dyadic cat (catenate). (Phase 3, Step 3.5)
  [x] 018-matrix-creation [legacy]: Extend rho for 2-element shapes creating rank-2 matrices. Row-major storage. (Phase 4, Step 4.1)
  [x] 019-matrix-display [legacy]: Matrix output with one row per line, right-justified aligned columns. (Phase 4, Step 4.2)
  [x] 020-matrix-ops [legacy]: Element-wise ops on matrices, scalar extension to matrices, rho/cat on matrices. (Phase 4, Step 4.3)
  [x] 021-matrix-take-drop [legacy]: take/drop on matrices operating on rows. (Phase 4, Step 4.4)
  [x] 022-system-commands [legacy]: Implement )CLEAR, )VARS, )OFF system commands. (Phase 5, Step 5.1)
  [x] 023-error-handling [legacy]: SYNTAX, LENGTH, RANK, DOMAIN, WS FULL, VALUE errors with recovery. (Phase 5, Step 5.2)
  [x] 024-edge-cases [legacy]: Empty vectors, single-element arrays, large arrays, nested parens, long lines. (Phase 5, Step 5.3)
  [x] 025-test-suite [legacy]: Comprehensive conformance test script with expected output comparison. (Phase 5, Step 5.4)
  [x] 026-final-docs [legacy]: Update README, document syntax and operations, example session, known limitations. (Phase 5, Step 5.5)
  [x] 027-027-quad-led [production]: Implement qled quad system variable for LED D2 hardware I/O. Add TOK_QLED to ...
  [x] 028-028-quad-sw [production]: Implement qsw quad system variable for switch S2 hardware I/O. Add TOK_QSW to...
  [x] 029-029-quad-exprs [production]: Verify quad variables work in arbitrary expression contexts. Test qled <- qsw...
  [x] 030-030-qsvo-system-fn [production]: Implement □SVO system function. Tokenizer recognizes qSVO as TOK_QSVO. Dyad...
  [x] 031-031-shared-var-indexing [production]: Implement indexed read/write on shared variables. MMIO[N] reads byte at FF000...
  [x] 032-032-graceful-degradation [production]: Implement graceful degradation pattern. Verify →(MMIORC<2)/NODEV branch-aro...
  [x] 033-033-bracket-indexing [production]: Implement bracket indexing on vectors. V[N] reads element N. V[N]<-expr write...
  [x] 034-034-bitwise-ops [production]: Implement bitwise operations: and (AND), or (OR), not (NOT). Reserved words l...
  [x] 035-035-branch-labels [production]: Implement branch (→) and labels. →N branches to line N, →LABEL to label...
  [x] 036-036-multiline-programs [production]: Implement multi-line program storage. Line array in heap. [N] expr syntax to ...
  [x] 037-037-user-functions [production]: Implement user-defined functions. del R<-FN X ... del (ASCII for ∇). Monadi...
  [x] 038-038-apl-image-format [production]: Define and implement APL image format. Newline-separated lines, null-terminat...
  [x] 039-039-batch-mode [production]: Implement batch mode detection and reader. Check known address for non-zero i...
  [x] 040-040-uart-echo-demo [production]: Build and test the full UART echo demo. APL batch program: echoes UART in to ...
  [x] 041-041-comparison-ops [production]: Comparison operators (= >= <= > < !=) returning 0/1 boolean vectors. Element-wise on vectors. Tokenize as new operator tokens, evaluate to integer 0 or 1. Test: 3 = 3 -> 1, 1 2 3 >= 2 -> 0 1 1. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 042-042-max-min [production]: Dyadic ceil (max) and floor (min) primitives. Add as reserved words. Extend reduce to support ceil/ and floor/ for max-reduce and min-reduce. Test: 3 ceil 5 -> 5, ceil/ 4 1 7 2 -> 7. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 043-043-compress [production]: Boolean compress operator. MASK compress VECTOR selects elements where mask is 1. New reserved word or syntax. Test: 1 0 1 compress 10 20 30 -> 10 30. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 044-044-char-type [production]: Character data type and string literals. Extend tokenizer for single-quoted strings. Store as char vectors in heap with type flag. Print char vectors as strings. Test: 'hello' prints hello. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 045-045-string-ops [production]: String operations: rho on characters (5 rho '#' -> '#####'), cat joining strings ('hi' cat ' world'). Extend existing rho and cat for character arrays. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 046-046-nested-arrays [production]: Boxed/nested arrays. Vector-of-strings creation via stranding: 'A' 'B' 'C'. Boxed storage where each element is a heap pointer. Nested vector display. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 047-047-pick [production]: Pick operator (pick) for nested vector indexing. I pick V returns the I-th boxed element. Test: 2 pick 'cat' 'dog' 'fish' -> 'fish'. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 048-048-prng-roll [production]: PRNG with LCG algorithm. Roll operator: roll N returns random 1..N, element-wise on vectors. qRL quad variable for seed. Auto-seed from UART keystroke timing. See docs/randomness.md. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 049-049-fmt-format [production]: Format operator (fmt). fmt N converts integer to character vector. fmt V converts vector to string. Used for: 'ROUND ' cat fmt N. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 050-050-quad-output [production]: Quad output (qout). qout <- expr prints value with newline. Needed for explicit output inside functions. Currently output is implicit from REPL eval. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 051-051-delay [production]: Delay function qDL. qDL N pauses for N milliseconds. Implement as calibrated spin loop or timer register read. Integer ms (no float). (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 052-052-comments [production]: Line comments. # at start of token position skips to end of line. Allows documenting APL programs. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 053-053-or-reduce [production]: Extend reduce for or/ (logical OR reduce) and and/ (logical AND reduce). Test: or/ 0 0 1 0 -> 1, and/ 1 1 0 -> 0. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [x] 054-054-horse-race-test [production]: Transliterate and run both horse race APL programs on COR24 emulator. Verify output matches GNU APL behavior. Milestone: full APL programs running on custom interpreter. (Horse race milestone)
  [x] 055-quad-io [production]: Implement []IO (quad index origin) system variable. Default value 1 (standard...

content.content:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
index e60a151..36f8bd8 100644
--- a/CLAUDE.md
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -29,8 +29,48 @@ Toolchain repos (siblings under `~/github/sw-embed/`):
 - `sw-cor24-tinyc` -- C compiler (tc24r)
 - `sw-cor24-emulator` -- emulator + assembler (cor24-run)
 
+## CRITICAL: Git Branching Workflow (devgroup policy)
+
+This clone is downstream of a coordinator-gated integration model:
+
+- `main` and `dev` are coordinator-only. **Never commit to them
+  directly, and never `git push`.** The coordinator (mike) relays
+  ready branches into `dev` and pushes.
+- Do all work on `feature/<topic>` or `fix/<topic>` branches, based
+  on local `dev` (which tracks the integration branch).
+- When work is ready for integration, rename the branch to
+  `pr/<topic>` so the coordinator's scan picks it up.
+- The ref name is the contract -- no PR API, no JSON, no tickets.
+
+### Helpers (on `PATH` via `$SCRIPTROOT`)
+
+```bash
+dg-new-feature <topic>   # switch dev, fetch, create feature/<topic>
+dg-new-fix <topic>       # same flavor, fix/<topic>
+dg-mark-pr               # rename current feature/* (or fix/*) -> pr/*
+dg-list-pr               # list pr/* branches
+dg-policy                # reprint this policy
+onboarding               # environment / state summary
+```
+
+### Rules
+
+- **Never `git push`** -- the coordinator handles all pushes.
+- Base new branches on local `dev`, not `main`.
+- Do not rewrite history on `dev` or `main`. Rebase is fine on your
+  own `feature/*` before marking `pr/*`.
+- After the coordinator integrates `pr/<topic>`, re-fetch and fast-
+  forward local `dev` before starting the next branch.
+
+Full policy:
+`/disk1/github/softwarewrighter/devgroup/docs/branching-pr-strategy.md`
+
 ## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly)
 
+Each AgentRail step maps to one `feature/<slug>` (or `fix/<slug>`)
+branch. Create the branch BEFORE doing the work, and rename it to
+`pr/<slug>` AFTER `agentrail complete`.
+
 ### 1. START (do this FIRST, before anything else)
 ```bash
 agentrail next
@@ -38,20 +78,27 @@ agentrail next
 Read the output carefully. It contains your current step, prompt,
 plan context, and any relevant skills/trajectories.
 
-### 2. BEGIN (immediately after reading the next output)
+### 2. BRANCH (create a work branch for the step)
+```bash
+dg-new-feature <slug>    # or dg-new-fix <slug> for a bug fix
+```
+Use the step's slug (e.g. `matrix-take-drop`) as the topic. This
+switches to `dev`, fetches, and creates `feature/<slug>`.
+
+### 3. BEGIN (tell AgentRail the step is started)
 ```bash
 agentrail begin
 ```
 
-### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says)
+### 4. WORK (do what the step prompt says)
 Do NOT ask "want me to proceed?". The step prompt IS your instruction.
 Execute it directly.
 
-### 4. COMMIT (after the work is done)
-Commit your code changes with git. Use `/mw-cp` for the checkpoint
-process (pre-commit checks, docs, detailed commit, push).
+### 5. COMMIT (after the work is done)
+Commit your code changes with git on the `feature/<slug>` branch.
---
  dev
* feature/claude-md-branching
  main
  remotes/origin/HEAD -> origin/main
  remotes/origin/main

content.text:
Right — I committed as `ee2a242` but you're correct on the analysis:

- Step 081's code (`7b5a9a4`) landed on `dev` directly (pre-policy), so there's no feature code left to branch.
- This `feature/claude-md-branching` branch now contains only the CLAUDE.md policy docs — it's docs, not a feature.
- `agentrail complete` is separate from git — just updates saga state.

So what's actually pending is two different things:

1. **AgentRail status cleanup** — run `agentrail complete` for step 081 (the code's already in dev). No branch needed for this; it's saga bookkeeping.
2. **CLAUDE.md docs delivery** — this commit needs to reach `dev` via the coordinator. The policy only allows `feature/*` or `fix/*` → `pr/*`, so naming it `feature/claude-md-branching` is fine (arguably `docs/*` would be cleaner but isn't in the policy).

Want me to:
- Run `agentrail complete` for step 081, then
- `dg-mark-pr` to rename this to `pr/claude-md-branching` for the coordinator?

Or hold off on either?

message.content: but all agentrail dirs and file are to be tracked and committed when changed.  If there are no more steps in the current saga, archive it.  That change needs to be committed.  Try to combine this into one pr branch at the end.  You can have more than one commit on a feature (saga-length) branch.  Only rename it when finished with all pending changes.  fixes are a special case of this and belong on their own branch.

content.content:
---

## Phase 9: Batch Mode

### Step 9.1: APL image format
- Newline-separated lines, null-terminated in memory
- Loaded via `cor24-run --load-binary program.apl@0x080000`
- Image pointer set via `--patch`

### Step 9.2: Mode detection and batch reader
- Interpreter checks known address for non-zero image pointer
- Batch mode: read lines from SRAM, not UART
- UART is free for the APL program to use via `□SVO`
- Interactive mode: unchanged REPL behavior
- Test: load simple APL program, verify execution

### Step 9.3: UART echo demo
- Full target use case: UART echo with LED/switch
- See `docs/apl-echo-example-plan.md` for the program listing
- Test via `cor24-run --terminal` with piped input
---
name = "cor24-apl-interpreter"

plan_file = ".agentrail/plan.md"

content.content:
=== PLAN ===
# COR24 APL Interpreter -- Implementation Plan

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Description                    | Deliverable                       |
|-------|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| 0     | Project setup and toolchain   | Build system, hello-world on emu  |
| 1     | Scalar REPL                   | Read, eval, print scalar exprs    |
| 2     | Vector support                | 1D arrays, element-wise ops       |
| 3     | Core APL primitives           | iota, rho, reduce, take, drop     |
| 4     | Matrix support                | 2D arrays, formatted output       |
| 5     | Polish and testing            | Error handling, edge cases, docs  |
| 6     | Shared variable I/O           | □SVO, AP 242, MMIO indexing       |
| 7     | Bracket indexing & bitwise    | V[N], and/or/not                  |
| 8     | Control flow & functions      | →, labels, ∇ user functions       |
| 9     | Batch mode                    | APL image loading, headless exec  |

---

## Phase 0: Project Setup and Toolchain Validation

### Step 0.1: Repository structure
- Create `src/` directory for C source files
- Create `asm/` directory for hand-written assembly helpers
- Create `test/` directory for test scripts
- Create `Makefile` or build script for tc24r + cor24-run pipeline
- Verify tc24r can compile a trivial C program to COR24 assembly
- Verify cor24-run can assemble and execute the result

### Step 0.2: UART I/O bootstrap
- Write `uart_putchar()` and `uart_getchar()` in C (or asm)
- Write `uart_puts()` for string output
- Write `uart_getline()` with backspace support
- Test: print "COR24 APL v0.1" on startup, echo typed lines

### Step 0.3: Number I/O
- Write `print_int()` -- integer to decimal string via UART
- Write `parse_int()` -- decimal string to integer
- Handle negative numbers (underscore prefix `_N`)

content.content:
Workflow CLI for keeping AI agents on track

Usage: agentrail [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  init            Initialize a new saga
  setup           Bootstrap a project: create saga, CLAUDE.md, and register domain
  add             Add a step to the saga (for maintenance mode or ad-hoc tasks)
  status          Show current saga state
  next            Output current step prompt and context for a fresh agent session
  begin           Mark the current step as in-progress
  complete        Complete current step, optionally define next step
  plan            View or update the saga plan
  history         Show all step summaries
  distill         Distill trajectories into a skill document
  run-loop        Auto-execute deterministic steps, pause at agent steps
  abort           Mark current step as blocked
  insert          Insert a new pending step at a chosen position, shifting later steps up
  reorder         Move an existing pending/in-progress step to a different position
  reopen          Reopen a completed or blocked step
  archive         Archive current saga to .agentrail-archive/ and clear .agentrail/
  audit           Compare git history with saga history, report gaps
  snapshot        Save a point-in-time copy of .agentrail/ into the git object store
  gen-agents-doc  Write AGENTS.example.md — an agent instructions template for agentrail
  help            Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --saga <SAGA>  Path to the project directory (default: current directory) [default: .]
  -h, --help         Print help
  -V, --version      Print version

  -h, --help         Print help
  -V, --version      Print version

content.content:
commit 7b5a9a4ecd2f09e0deaf58582ecfdb07ff839f55
Author: Mike Wright <mike@softwarewrighter.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 5 09:20:25 2026 -0700

    feat(ops): multi-axis take and drop on matrices (Step 081)
    
    Extend take and drop to accept 2-element vector left args for matrices:
    - R C take M: take R rows and C columns
    - R C drop M: drop R rows and C columns
    - Negative values take/drop from the end
    - Scalar left arg still works (rows only, existing behavior)
    
    Examples: 2 2 take 3 3 rho iota 9 -> 2x2 top-left corner
              _1 _1 drop 3 3 rho iota 9 -> 2x2 top-left (same)
    
    All 38 tests pass.
    
    Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 .agentrail/saga.toml                               |    2 +-
 .../b302930d-6931-4a4b-abdf-099eefae4b26.jsonl     | 1213 ++++++++++++++++++++
 .agentrail/steps/066-066-rotate/step.toml          |    3 +-

 .agentrail/steps/067-067-transpose/step.toml       |    3 +-
 .agentrail/steps/067-067-transpose/summary.md      |    1 +
 .agentrail/steps/068-068-encode-decode/step.toml   |    3 +-
 .agentrail/steps/068-068-encode-decode/summary.md  |    1 +

 .agentrail/steps/069-069-enclose/summary.md        |    1 +
 .agentrail/steps/070-070-outer-product/step.toml   |    3 +-

input.command: agentrail complete --summary "Extended take and drop primitives to accept 2-element vector left arg for matrices, enabling multi-axis slicing (e.g. 2 3 take M, _1 _1 drop M). Scalar left arg path preserved; negative counts slice from the trailing end. All 38 batch tests pass." --reward 1 --actions "Edited src/ops.c to branch on left-arg shape, reused row/column stride math, added .apl and .a24 fixtures, ran ./build.sh run and test harness." --done 2>&1 | tail -20
